services.title: Birth Certificate
services.description: Get a certified copy of birth certificate registered in your city
services.seoDescription: Get a certified copy of birth certificate registered in {{lguName}}.

detail.description: Official certified copy of birth certificate registered in your city

processSteps.title: Check Eligibility
processSteps.description: You can request if you are the owner, parent/guardian, or authorized representative with proper authorization letter.

processSteps.title: Prepare Documents
processSteps.description: Bring valid government-issued ID. If representative: authorization letter and IDs of both owner and representative.

processSteps.title: Visit Civil Registry
processSteps.description: Go to the City Civil Registry Office - Window 4 at 1st Floor, Administrative Building. Office hours: Mon-Fri, 8AM-5PM.

processSteps.title: Fill Out Request Form
processSteps.description: Complete the request form / verification slip with full name, date of birth, place of birth, parents' names, and purpose.

processSteps.title: Pay the Fee
processSteps.description: Pay ₱75 per certified copy at Window 2 or 3 of the Civil Registry or at the City Treasurer's Office (same building).

processSteps.title: Claim Certificate
processSteps.description: Return to Civil Registry Window 4 with official receipt. Certificate released within 15-30 minutes if record is available.

requirements.title: If You Are a Representative
items: Authorization letter signed by the owner
Valid ID of the representative (original + photocopy)

faqs.question: Can I get a birth certificate registered in another city?
faqs.answer: No. The City Civil Registry only has records for births registered in this city. For other cities, request from that city's Civil Registry or from PSA.

faqs.question: What if I don't know the exact date of birth?
faqs.answer: Provide an approximate year and month. The staff will help search the records, but this may take longer.

faqs.question: Can I request through email or online?
faqs.answer: Currently, requests must be made in person. Online services are being planned for the future.

faqs.question: What's the difference between local copy and PSA copy?
faqs.answer: Both are certified true copies. PSA copies are nationally-recognized and required for passport/visa applications. Local copies are accepted for most local transactions and school enrollment.

office.name: City Civil Registry
office.location: 1st Floor, Administrative Building, Las Piñas City Hall Compound

services.title: Marriage Certificate
services.description: Register or request certified true copy of marriage certificate
services.seoDescription: Register or request certified true copy of marriage certificate in {{lguName}}.

detail.description: Register or request certified true copy of marriage certificate

processSteps.title: Gather Documents
processSteps.description: Prepare marriage certificate from solemnizing officer and valid IDs.

processSteps.title: Visit Civil Registry
processSteps.description: Go to the Civil Registry Office, 1st Floor, Administrative Building.

processSteps.title: Submit Documents & Verification
processSteps.description: Submit marriage certificate and supporting documents for registration at Window 5. Staff verifies document authenticity and completeness.

processSteps.title: Payment
processSteps.description: Pay the Solemnization Fee of ₱150 at Window 2 or 3 in the Civil Registry Office.

processSteps.title: Registration
processSteps.description: Marriage is officially registered in the Civil Registry.

processSteps.title: Claim Certificate
processSteps.description: Receive certified copy of registered marriage certificate.

requirements.title: For Timely Registration
items: Duly accomplished Certificate of Marriage - 4 copies
Marriage solemnized outside Churches or Municipal Court must submit a Request address to solemnizing officer for celebration of Marriage in a place other than those authorized by law

requirements.title: For Late Registration
items: Duly accomplished Certificate of Marriage - 4 copies
Certificate of Marriage of contracting parties

requirements.title: For Copy Request
items: Valid ID of spouse or authorized representative

faqs.question: How soon after the wedding should I register?
faqs.answer: Within 30 days of the marriage ceremony. Late registration may require additional documents.

faqs.question: Can I get a copy if I was married elsewhere?
faqs.answer: Only marriages registered in this city are available. Request from the city where you were married or from PSA.

office.name: City Civil Registry
office.location: 1st Floor, Administrative Building, Las Piñas City Hall Compound

services.title: Death Certificate
services.description: Register death certificate and obtain burial or transfer permit
services.seoDescription: Register death certificate and obtain burial or transfer permit in {{lguName}}.

detail.description: Register death certificate and obtain burial/transfer permit

processSteps.title: Obtain Medical Certificate
processSteps.description: Get certificate of death from attending physician or hospital.

processSteps.title: Visit Civil Registry
processSteps.description: Go to Civil Registry at Window 5 with death certificate within 48 hours.

processSteps.title: Complete Registry
processSteps.description: Staff registers death in civil registry.

processSteps.title: Pay Fees
processSteps.description: Pay registration of ₱75, ₱150 if late, and burial permit fees at Window 2 or 3 in the Civil Registry Office or at the City Treasurer's Office.

processSteps.title: Claim Permits
processSteps.description: Receive burial permit and certified death certificate at Window 5.

requirements.title: For Late Registration
items: Negative Certification from Philippine Statistics Authority
Duly accomplished Certificate of Death or Certificate of Fetal Death

faqs.question: How soon must death be registered?
faqs.answer: Within 48 hours of death. Late registration requires additional documents and fees.

faqs.question: Do I need a burial permit?
faqs.answer: Yes, burial permit is required before interment in any cemetery.

faqs.question: How long is the posting period for Late Registration?
faqs.answer: Evaluator shall prepare documents for posting within 10 days, and the release of Certified True Copy is expiration of 10 days posting period.

office.name: City Civil Registry
office.location: 1st Floor, Administrative Building, Las Piñas City Hall Compound

services.title: Barangay Clearance
services.description: Certificate of residence from your barangay

services.title: Barangay ID
services.description: Official barangay identification card

services.title: Police Clearance
services.description: Police clearance coordination through city office

services.title: Business Permit (New)
services.description: Apply for a new mayor's permit to operate a business
services.seoDescription: Apply for a new Business License and Mayor's Permit in {{lguName}}.

detail.fullTitle: Issuance of New Business License and Mayor's Permit
detail.description: Apply for a new mayor's permit to operate a business within the city as per City Ordinance No: 1373-16

processSteps.title: Submit Application
processSteps.description: Submit duly accomplished Unified Application Form together with complete requirements to BPLO.

processSteps.title: Planning & Engineering Evaluation
processSteps.description: Personnel from Planning (Zoning/Locational Clearance) and Engineering (Occupancy) evaluate the application at the BPLO office.

processSteps.title: Data Verification
processSteps.description: Verification of accounts and issuance of a Business Account Number at the Electronic Data Processing (EDP) division.

processSteps.title: Approval
processSteps.description: The application is forwarded for approval by the Chief of BPLO or their authorized representative.

processSteps.title: Generate Payment Order
processSteps.description: Release of the signed Tax Order of Payment (TOP) covering consolidated taxes, fees, and regulatory charges.

processSteps.title: Pay Taxes and Fees
processSteps.description: Present TOP to the Business License Tax Division, Treasury Department and pay assessed fees. An Official Receipt and Cedula will be issued.

processSteps.title: Claim Permit & Clearances
processSteps.description: Claim your Business License, Mayor's Permit, Sanitary Permit, and other clearances at BPLO.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Proof of Legal Ownership (Tax Dec, TCT) or Notarized Contract of Lease
Picture of Business Establishment (3R Size Colored, 1pc)
Special Requirements based on line of business (if applicable)

requirements.title: If Applied Through Representative
items: Letter of Authority / Special Power of Attorney / Secretary Certificate
Valid ID of owner and representative

faqs.question: How long does the processing take?
faqs.answer: As prescribed by R.A. 11032 (Ease of Doing Business Law), government transactions follow a 3-7-20 day rule. While typical applications are processed within 1 Day, official timelines are: Up to 3 days for Simple, 7 days for Complex, and 20 days for Highly Technical transactions. If City Council approval is required, it may take 45 working days (extendable by 20 days).

faqs.question: What if that location had a previous tenant?
faqs.answer: The previous tenant must have filed a formal Closure/Retirement of business to the City Treasurer's Office. Otherwise, the building owner must submit a Termination of Lease Contract and an Affidavit of No Relation so the new applicant can secure an account number.

services.title: Business Permit Renewal
services.description: Renew your annual business permit
services.seoDescription: Renew your annual Business License and Mayor's Permit in {{lguName}}.

detail.fullTitle: Renewal of Business License and Mayor's Permit
detail.description: Renew your annual business permit, Mayor's clearance, and licensing services as per City Ordinance No: 1373-16

processSteps.title: Submit Application
processSteps.description: Submit duly accomplished application form indicating gross sale/receipt together with complete requirements to BPLO.

processSteps.title: Evaluation & Approval
processSteps.description: BPLO personnel reviews requirements. The application is then forwarded for approval by the BPLO Chief or authorized representative.

processSteps.title: Generate Payment Order
processSteps.description: Once approved, a computer-generated Tax Order of Payment (TOP) is released, covering consolidated taxes and fees.

processSteps.title: Pay Taxes and Fees
processSteps.description: Present TOP and pay at the Business License Division, Treasury Department. Receive Official Receipt and Cedula.

processSteps.title: Claim Permit & Clearances
processSteps.description: Return to BPLO to claim your Business License, Mayor's Permit, Sanitary Permit, and other regulatory clearances.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Prior Year Audited Financial Statement / ITR (Last 2 years)
Monthly/Quarterly VAT Returns / Percentage Tax Returns of previous years
Special Requirements based on line of business (if applicable)

requirements.title: If Applied Through Representative
items: Letter of Authority / Special Power of Attorney / Secretary Certificate
Valid ID of owner and representative

faqs.question: When should I renew my business permit?
faqs.answer: Before January 20 of each year to avoid penalties.

faqs.question: How long does the processing take?
faqs.answer: According to the Citizen's Charter 2022, the total processing time for a simple renewal is approximately 3 hours and 10 minutes.

faqs.question: What happens if I renew late?
faqs.answer: Renewals after the January 20 deadline are subject to surcharges and monthly interest as prescribed by the City's Revenue Code (Ordinance 1373-16).

services.title: Special Permit
services.description: Apply for a Special Permit to operate a business
services.seoDescription: Apply for a Special Permit to operate your business establishment in {{lguName}}.

detail.description: Special Permit issued by the City Government to all business establishment that operates within its territorial jurisdiction pursuant to City Ordinance No: 1373 - 16.

processSteps.title: Submit Application & Verification
processSteps.description: Submit accomplished application form and complete requirements to the BPLO front liners for receiving, reviewing, and validating. Evaluated by Planning Department for Zoning Fees. Passed for approval of the Chief of BPLO, who issues a Tax Order Payment (TOP) routing you to the City Treasurer's.

processSteps.title: Pay Taxes and Fees
processSteps.description: Present the TOP to the Business License Tax Miscellaneous Division. Taxes are calculated based on the Revenue Code. Payment is accepted and Official Receipt is printed/issued.

processSteps.title: Claim Special Permit
processSteps.description: Return to Business Permit & Licensing Office (BPLO) for the processing, printing, signing, and releasing of the Special Permit.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Business Name Registration (1 Copy: DTI Registration or SEC Registration & Articles of Incorporation)
Proof of Legal Ownership: Tax Declaration/TCT, Notarized Contract of Lease, or Letter of Authority to use property
Picture of Owner / President or Treasurer
Homeowners / Subdivision / Villages Clearance or Condominium Approval to conduct business

requirements.title: If Applied Through Representative
items: Letter of Authority / Special Power of Attorney / Secretary Certificate

faqs.question: How long does the processing take?
faqs.answer: According to the Citizen's Charter, the total processing time for a Special Permit is approximately 2 hours and 10 minutes.

faqs.question: What if that location had a previous tenant?
faqs.answer: The previous tenant must have filed a formal Closure/Retirement of business to the City Treasurer's Office. Otherwise, the building owner must submit a Termination of Lease Contract and an Affidavit of No Relation so the new applicant can secure an account number.

services.title: Certificates on Status of Business
services.description: Certification on the status of business entity registered in Las Piñas
services.seoDescription: Verify the status of a business entity registered with the BPLO in {{lguName}}.

detail.fullTitle: Issuance of Certificates on Status of Business
detail.description: Certification issued by the City Government to all who want to verify the status of a business entity registered with the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO). Information access is limited pursuant to the Data Privacy Act of 2012 to maintain record integrity.

processSteps.title: Submit Application & Requirements
processSteps.description: Fill out the application form and submit the request letter together with other required documents to the BPLO frontliners for review and validation. The BPLO will verify records and issue an Order of Payment.

processSteps.title: Pay the Fee
processSteps.description: Proceed to the Office of the City Treasurer's – Miscellaneous Division and pay ₱75, plus ₱15 per additional page. An Official Receipt will be issued.

processSteps.title: Claim the Certification
processSteps.description: Return to the BPLO for the processing and printing of the Certification, signing by the Chief of BPLO, and release to the applicant.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Request Letter indicating the name of requestor and purpose of certification
Referral letter from Hospital/DSWD/PAO or other government agencies (if applicable)

requirements.title: If Applying Through a Representative
items: Letter of Authority, Special Power of Attorney, or Secretary Certificate
Valid ID of both the owner and representative

faqs.question: What is the purpose of this certification?
faqs.answer: This certification is used to verify the legal status of a business entity registered in Las Piñas City. It is accepted for legal, government, and private transactions.

faqs.question: Why is access to business records limited?
faqs.answer: Pursuant to the Data Privacy Act of 2012 (RA 10173), the BPLO limits the data that can be accessed to protect the integrity and privacy of the information in its records.

services.title: Certified True Copy of Business License and Mayor's Permit
services.description: Certified Copy of Business License and Mayor's Permit for legal purposes
services.seoDescription: Request a Certified True Copy of Business License and Mayor's Permit in {{lguName}}.

detail.fullTitle: Issuance of Certified True Copy of Business License and Mayor's Permit
detail.description: Certified Copy of Business License and Mayor's Permit issued by the City Government to Business Owners who request it in whatever legal purposes it may serve.

processSteps.title: Submit Requirements
processSteps.description: Submit the requirements to the BPLO frontliners for review, validation, and verification of records. An Order of Payment will be issued.

processSteps.title: Pay the Fee
processSteps.description: Upon receiving the Order of Payment, pay ₱90 at the Office of the City Treasurer's – Miscellaneous Division. An Official Receipt will be issued.

processSteps.title: Claim the Document
processSteps.description: Return to the BPLO for the stamping, signing, and releasing of the Certified True Copy of Business License and Mayor's Permit.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Photocopy of Business License and Mayor's Permit

requirements.title: If Applying Through a Representative
items: Letter of Authority, Special Power of Attorney, or Secretary Certificate

faqs.question: What is the purpose of this certified true copy?
faqs.answer: It is issued by the City Government to Business Owners who request it for whatever legal purposes it may serve.

services.title: Occupational Mayor's Permit (Regular)
services.description: Apply for an Occupational Mayor's Permit for workers or employees
services.seoDescription: Secure an Occupational Mayor's Permit for workers or employees in {{lguName}}.

detail.description: The Occupational Mayor's Permit is issued by the City Government to all workers or employees (temporary or permanent) employed within the territorial jurisdiction of Las Piñas, pursuant to the local Revenue Code.

processSteps.title: Secure Application Form
processSteps.description: Secure and fill out the application form at the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO).

processSteps.title: Submit Requirements
processSteps.description: Submit the application form along with the required documents such as Cedula and NBI/Police Clearance for evaluation.

processSteps.title: Assessment and Payment
processSteps.description: Proceed through the assessment and pay the assessed fees as directed by the BPLO personnel.

processSteps.title: Claim Permit
processSteps.description: Claim the Occupational Mayor's Permit once processed and approved.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Application Form (2 copies - secured from BPLO)

requirements.title: For First-Time Jobseekers (R.A. 11261)
items: Barangay Certificate with an undertaking stating you are a first-time job seeker to avail the assistance.

faqs.question: What is the processing time for the permit?
faqs.answer: The BPLO classifies the issuance of the Occupational Mayor's Permit as a simple transaction, typically taking around 3 hours depending on the volume of applicants.

faqs.question: Do I need to apply in person?
faqs.answer: While in-person application is standard, the city has been integrating services online. Please check the official Las Piñas City website for recent updates.

relatedServices.title: Occupational Mayor's Permit (First Time Job Seeker)

services.title: Occupational Mayor's Permit (First Time Job Seeker)
services.description: Free Occupational Mayor's Permit for first-time job seekers (R.A. 11261)
services.seoDescription: Avail the free Occupational Mayor's Permit for First Time Job Seekers in {{lguName}} under RA 11261.

detail.fullTitle: Occupational Mayor's Permit (First Time Job Seeker)
detail.description: The City Government of Las Piñas provides assistance to "First Time Job Seekers" regarding the issuance of an Occupational Mayor's Permit, in compliance with Republic Act No. 11261, the "First Time Jobseekers Assistance Act" of 2019.

processSteps.title: Secure Application Form
processSteps.description: Secure and fill out the application form at the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO).

processSteps.title: Submit Requirements
processSteps.description: Submit the application form along with the required documents such as Cedula, NBI/Police Clearance, and your Barangay Certificate with First-time job seeker undertaking.

processSteps.title: Assessment and Evaluation
processSteps.description: Proceed through the assessment where personnel will evaluate your requirements and check your Barangay Certificate to waive local fees.

processSteps.title: Claim Permit
processSteps.description: Claim the Occupational Mayor's Permit once processed and approved.

requirements.title: Documentary Requirements
items: Barangay Certificate with an undertaking stating you are a first-time job seeker (RA 11261)
Application Form (2 copies - secured from BPLO)

requirements.title: Additional Medical Requirements
items: Urine and Stool test results (specifically required for food handlers)

faqs.question: Who is considered a First Time Job Seeker?
faqs.answer: A First Time Job Seeker is defined as a Filipino citizen looking for work for the first time, who is between 15 to 30 years old, and not currently receiving benefits from other government job programs.

faqs.question: Are all fees waived under RA 11261?
faqs.answer: Yes, government regulatory fees for standard documents such as the Occupational Permit, Barangay Clearance, and NBI Clearance are free of charge for valid first-time job seekers.

faqs.question: What if I am a food handler?
faqs.answer: If you are applying as a food handler, you will be required to submit specific test results (Urine and Stool) and also undergo a seminar at the Sanitation Office.

faqs.question: What assistance programs are available?

faqs.question: Who can apply for financial assistance?
faqs.answer: Indigent families and individuals in crisis situations.

services.title: Safety Seal Certificate
services.description: Certification for business establishments following Minimum Public Health Standards (MPHS)
services.seoDescription: Apply for a Safety Seal Certificate to certify your business establishment's compliance with health standards in {{lguName}}.

detail.description: Certification issued by the City Government to ensure that all business establishments follow Minimum Public Health Standards (MPHS) and promote business without compromising public health.

processSteps.title: Submit Application
processSteps.description: Submit the duly accomplished Safety Seal Certification Form along with attached eligibility requirements to the BPLO.

processSteps.title: Review & Scheduling
processSteps.description: BPLO personnel will review and validate the requirements submitted and schedule the date of inspection.

processSteps.title: Field Inspection
processSteps.description: Inspectors from the BPLO and related divisions will inspect the business establishment to validate compliance with health standards.

processSteps.title: Database Recording
processSteps.description: The inspected establishment is encoded into the database for recording and tracking.

processSteps.title: Issuance of Certificate
processSteps.description: The Safety Seal Certificate is issued and sent to the applicant's registered email address.

requirements.title: Application Documents
items: Duly Accomplished Safety Seal Certification Form
Valid Business License and Mayor's Permit

requirements.title: Health & Safety Protocols
items: Use of StaySafe.ph or integrated contact tracing tool
Availability of thermal scanners for employees and clients
Health declaration sheets for individuals entering the premises
Designated isolation area for symptomatic employees
BHERT and COVID-19 hotlines placed in conspicuous areas
Hand washing stations with soap and sanitizers
Physical barriers and floor markers for social distancing
Windows for adequate air exchange in indoor areas

requirements.title: Compliance & Personnel
items: Availability of personnel-in-charge for protocol monitoring
Designated Safety Officer for contact tracing and referral
Established referral system for medical and psychosocial services
Regular disinfection protocol (at least twice a week)
Mandatory wearing of face masks and shields
Storage facility for used facemasks and infectious waste

faqs.question: Is there a fee for the Safety Seal?
faqs.answer: No, the issuance of the Safety Seal Certificate is free of charge as part of the city's effort to support safe business operations.

faqs.question: How long is the processing time?
faqs.answer: The total processing time from submission to issuance via email is 7 working days.
